sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Shoulder dystocia is an obstetric emergency in which the shoulders fail to deliver after the head. The priorities are immediate recognition, urgent help, calm sequencing of manoeuvres, and avoidance of harmful traction or fundal pressure.

sections.title: HELPERR Manoeuvres
sections.content:
1. Call for urgent assistance and clearly declare the dystocia

2. Ask the mother to stop pushing between directed manoeuvres if possible

3. Start with McRoberts positioning and suprapubic pressure; do not apply fundal pressure

4. If these fail, proceed through internal rotational manoeuvres, delivery of the posterior arm, or position change only within training and scope

5. Continue in a calm, deliberate sequence and prepare for immediate neonatal resuscitation after delivery

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Do not apply fundal pressure because it worsens impaction
- Do not pull on the baby's head or neck in an attempt to force delivery
- McRoberts and suprapubic pressure should happen early and clearly
- Document the time of head delivery, sequence of manoeuvres, full delivery time, and neonatal condition
